label: Python module (one source file)
gloss: A Python module: one source file of Python code, the unit the parser reads as a whole.
notes: Structural (non-NSM) definition; the NSM explication of code constructs is the idea-A sub-programme (reports/lit-structured-parsing-and-inner-symbolic.md thread A). Identity is the definition object below, content-addressed.

definition.definition: A Python module: one source file of Python source text, parsed by the CPython standard-library ast parser as a single ast.Module node. Within a pinned corpus a module is identified by its file path relative to the corpus root. A module's body is the ordered sequence of its top-level statements.
